title: Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi
tagline: Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Ranganatha Swamy Temple at Nirthadi (also spelt Neerthadi or Niratadi), is a post-Vijayanagara Empire re-construction.
overview.description: The Ranganatha Swamy Temple at Nirthadi (also spelt Neerthadi or Niratadi), is a post-Vijayanagara Empire re-construction. Nirthadi is a village in the Davangere district of Karnataka state, India. According to noted historian and epigraphist Benjamin Lewis Rice, a Kannada language inscription dated 1698 AD in the temple premise describes the destruction of the original temple by the armies of Mogul emperor Aurangzeb in 1696 AD. The Chitradurga chief Baramappa Nayaka (r.1689–1721) rebuilt the temple in 1698 AD. The monument is protected by the Karnataka state division of Archaeological Survey

topPlaces.description: Sri Taralabalu Jagadguru Brihanmath is an organization in Sirigere, India. Sadar Lingayats, a dominant sub sect of Lingayat community, owe their allegiance to this math.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (120 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (155 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi?
faq.a: Stays in Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ranganathaswamy Temple, Nirthadi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Taralabalu Jagadguru Brihanmath, Sirigere.
